detail.title: Fan-out Audit Agents — Parallel Review Across Three Axes

consumes.artifact: `.arcforge-epic` marker files and worktree directory presence under `~/.arcforge/worktrees/` (file-level observation only — no git commands) for the state-transition-integrity agent

produces.artifact: Structured finding lists returned to the main session via Task-tool responses (no filesystem output)

requirement.title: Parallel fan-out to three axis-aligned sub-agents
requirement.description: Phase 1 of the skill body MUST spawn exactly three sub-agents in parallel via the Task tool, one per audit axis. Each sub-agent runs in fresh context with zero visibility into the parent session's prior messages.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given Phase 1 begins, When the skill body fires fan-out, Then it MUST spawn exactly three sub-agents — `cross-artifact-alignment`, `internal-consistency`, and `state-transition-integrity` — concurrently in a single Task-tool message with multiple parallel tool uses. design.md §Architecture — Phase 1 Fan-out

Given each sub-agent is spawned, When its prompt is assembled, Then it MUST include the target spec-id plus explicit absolute paths (or explicit absence markers) for design.md, spec.xml, details/*.xml, and dag.yaml, so the agent can read them via its Read tool without guessing paths. design.md §Requirements Part 1 — Fan-out

requirement.title: Structured finding schema
requirement.description: Every finding emitted by any sub-agent MUST conform to a structured schema carrying identity, severity, diagnostic prose, and up to four suggested resolutions — each resolution carrying an optional diff-preview string for downstream AskUserQuestion rendering.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a sub-agent produces a finding, When the finding is emitted, Then it MUST include: a unique ID in format `A<axis>-<NNN>` (axis ∈ {1, 2, 3}, NNN zero-padded), a severity in {HIGH, MED, LOW, INFO}, a one-line title, a list of affected file paths (with line references when known), an "observed" prose block, a "why it matters" paragraph, and up to four suggested resolutions. design.md §Requirements Part 1 — Finding structure

Given a finding carries one or more resolutions, When each resolution is serialised, Then it MUST include a short label (1-5 words), a description of its effect, and — when the resolution corresponds to an editable artifact change — a `preview` diff string suitable for AskUserQuestion's preview field. Resolutions without a direct editable-artifact equivalent (e.g., engine-fix recommendations) MAY omit `preview`. design.md §Requirements Part 1 — Finding structure

Given the reviewer agent has a clear preference among its resolutions, When resolutions are ordered, Then the first resolution MUST be prefixed `"(Recommended)"`; when no preference exists, the prefix MUST NOT be applied to any resolution. design.md §Requirements Part 1 — Finding structure

Given severity is assigned to a finding, When severity taxonomy is interpreted, Then severity is drawn from a four-value enum {HIGH, MED, LOW, INFO}. HIGH / MED / LOW are reviewer-agent judgment calls — specific cut-off criteria MUST live in each agent's system prompt (produced via arc-writing-skills implementation), not in this spec. INFO is reserved for findings that are informational rather than issue-raising (e.g., graceful-degradation notices per fr-aa-004); it MUST NOT be used for downgraded HIGH / MED / LOW issues. This spec asserts only the presence of a severity field drawn from this four-value enum, not the HIGH / MED / LOW semantic thresholds. Refiner clarifying question Q1 → answer (b): loose spec definition, detail deferred to implementation

requirement.title: Axis-scope separation among the three agents
requirement.description: Each of the three sub-agents MUST cover a distinct audit axis. Findings outside an agent's axis MUST NOT appear in that agent's output.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given the `cross-artifact-alignment` agent runs, When its findings are inspected, Then every emitted finding MUST address semantic alignment between two or more of `design.md`, `spec.xml`, or `dag.yaml` — not an issue internal to a single artifact. design.md §Architecture — Cross-Artifact Alignment axis

Given the `internal-consistency` agent runs, When its findings are inspected, Then every emitted finding MUST address a contradiction, dangling reference, or self-referential prose within a single artifact (e.g., requirement contradicting a `<consumes>` entry inside the same spec.xml, or rename source/target identity within design.md). design.md §Architecture — Internal Consistency axis

Given the `state-transition-integrity` agent runs, When its findings are inspected, Then every emitted finding MUST address whether `dag.yaml` recorded state (epic status, worktree pointer, feature status) matches on-disk worktree directory presence, `.arcforge-epic` marker files, and feature-status files — not the semantic content of upstream artifacts and not git-history-layer drift (the latter is out of scope for this skill and belongs to a separate engine-fix spec). design.md §Architecture — State Transition Integrity axis

requirement.title: Graceful degradation on missing or failed inputs
requirement.description: Each sub-agent MUST degrade gracefully when an expected artifact is missing or when its own execution fails partially. The skill body MUST NOT abort the full audit because one axis cannot complete.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given `specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml` does not exist at skill invocation (pure design-stage audit), When `cross-artifact-alignment` runs, Then it MUST skip alignment-with-spec checks and emit exactly one finding with `severity: INFO` that records the skip and its cause. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Graceful degradation

Given `specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml` does not exist at skill invocation, When `state-transition-integrity` runs, Then it MUST return exactly one finding with `severity: INFO` and title "DAG not yet planned — state integrity not applicable" rather than running further checks. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Graceful degradation

Given any sub-agent experiences a partial failure (token-limit exhaustion, malformed input, tool error), When the agent returns to the orchestrator, Then it MUST return any partial findings already collected plus an explicit `error_flag` field describing the failure cause; the skill body MUST continue rendering Phases 2-5 using findings from axes that succeeded. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Graceful degradation
